Variables.Name: LightGuid
Variables.Description:
GUID used to associate a light component with precomputed shadowing information across levels.
	  The GUID changes whenever the light position changes.

Variables.Name: Intensity
Variables.Description:
Total energy that the light emits.  
	  For pointspot lights with inverse squared falloff, this is in units of lumens.  1700 lumens corresponds to a 100W lightbulb. 
	  For other lights, this is just a brightness multiplier.

Variables.Name: LightColor
Variables.Description:
Filter color of the light.
	  Note that this can change the light's effective intensity.

Variables.Name: bAffectsWorld
Variables.Description:
Whether the light can affect the world, or whether it is disabled.
	  A disabled light will not contribute to the scene in any way.  This setting cannot be changed at runtime and unbuilds lighting when changed.
	  Setting this to false has the same effect as deleting the light, so it is useful for non-destructive experiments.

Variables.Name: CastShadows
Variables.Description: Whether the light should cast any shadows.

Variables.Name: CastStaticShadows
Variables.Description: Whether the light should cast shadows from static objects.  Also requires Cast Shadows to be set to True.

Variables.Name: CastDynamicShadows
Variables.Description: Whether the light should cast shadows from dynamic objects.  Also requires Cast Shadows to be set to True.

Variables.Name: bAffectTranslucentLighting
Variables.Description: Whether the light affects translucency or not.  Disabling this can save GPU time when there are many small lights.

Variables.Name: bCastVolumetricShadow
Variables.Description: Whether the light shadows volumetric fog.  Disabling this can save GPU time.

Variables.Name: IndirectLightingIntensity
Variables.Description:
Scales the indirect lighting contribution from this light. 
	  A value of 0 disables any GI from this light. Default is 1.

Variables.Name: VolumetricScatteringIntensity
Variables.Description: Intensity of the volumetric scattering from this light.  This scales Intensity and LightColor.

Functions.Name: SetCastShadows
Functions.Description: Sets whether this light casts shadows

Functions.Name: GetLightColor
Functions.Description: Gets the light color as a linear color
